findings.title: Use of console and stderr instead of structured logging
findings.summary: State handling and dispatch steps write directly to console.log/warn or process.stderr, bypassing structured logging and omitting context like runId or timestamps.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:384 - `console.log("Reusing existing document result...")` skips the logger.
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:610 - Uses `process.stderr.write` to report worktree creation errors instead of emitting a telemetry event.
packages/remediate-code/src/state/store.ts:148 - Uses `console.warn` for lock closure failures instead of a context-aware logger.

findings.title: Silently swallowed errors drop observability context
findings.summary: Multiple try-catch blocks silently swallow errors (e.g., JSON parse failures, file access errors) without recording them in telemetry, making it difficult to observe why operations failed.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:53 - `catch { return false; }` in `tryLoadExistingDocumentResult` drops errors from `readJsonFile` or validation without any debug logs.
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/intakeResolver.ts - `shouldTryAuditFastPath` has a `catch { parsed = undefined; }` that swallows JSON parsing errors for input files, hiding malformed input cases.

findings.title: Insufficient context on thrown errors
findings.summary: Validation and state loading logic throws generic Errors without including contextual diagnostic details that would aid in troubleshooting.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:338 - `throw new Error("Cannot prepare document dispatch without plan and items.")` doesn't include the run ID or state status.
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:641 - `throw new Error("Implement worker result must be an object.")` lacks context about which result path or task caused the validation failure.

findings.title: Silently swallowed errors drop observability context
findings.summary: Multiple operations swallow errors silently without generating logs or telemetry. This conceals failures in quota file reading, worktree management, and file hashing.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/waveScheduler.ts - `catch { // Best-effort: proceed without learned state }` in `scheduleWave` drops `readQuotaState` errors without recording the telemetry that quota persistence failed.

packages/remediate-code/src/steps/worktreeIsolation.ts - Catch blocks like `catch { /* best effort */ }` and `catch { /* branch might not exist */ }` suppress potential edge cases during worktree creation and removal, dropping diagnostics from trace logs.

packages/remediate-code/src/utils/fileIntegrity.ts - `hashFileSync` and `hashFile` silently return `undefined` on catch, burying unexpected FS errors (like permission denied) that affect file hashing.
